name: wrangler
description: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices.
capabilities: Generate Wrangler CLI commands for deploying Workers
Create configuration files for KV, R2, D1, Vectorize bindings
Provide local development commands with wrangler dev
Explain secret management with wrangler secret
List available Workers AI models and configurations
author: cloudflare
securitySummary: All 161 static findings are false positives. The skill is official Cloudflare documentation for the Wrangler CLI tool. External command detections are legitimate CLI commands documented in code blocks. Network detections are official Cloudflare URLs and localhost testing endpoints. Environment variable access is standard secret management documentation. No malicious behavior detected.
valueStatement: Cloudflare Workers deployment requires understanding of CLI commands, configuration, and resource bindings. This skill provides comprehensive documentation for deploying and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, and more.
userTitle: Deploy Cloudflare Workers with Wrangler CLI
limitations: Does not execute commands - provides documentation only
Cannot access user Cloudflare account or credentials
Does not provide real-time deployment status

useCases.title: Deploy a new Worker to Cloudflare
useCases.description: Get step-by-step guidance for initializing and deploying a new Worker with proper configuration

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers adding storage to Workers
useCases.title: Configure KV and R2 storage bindings
useCases.description: Learn how to set up key-value storage and object storage for your Worker

useCases.title: Manage secrets for production Workers
useCases.description: Securely manage API keys and secrets using wrangler secret commands

promptTemplates.title: Basic Worker deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I deploy my Worker to Cloudflare using wrangler?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to deploy a Worker for the first time

promptTemplates.title: Configure KV namespace
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to add a KV namespace binding to my wrangler.jsonc config
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to add persistent key-value storage

promptTemplates.title: Set up secrets
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I securely add API keys as secrets for my Worker?
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to configure sensitive credentials

promptTemplates.title: Local development with bindings
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I test my Worker locally with remote R2 and AI bindings?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to test integration with cloud resources

bestPractices: Run wrangler check before every deployment to catch configuration errors
Use wrangler types after any config changes to regenerate TypeScript bindings
Set compatibility_date to a recent date within 30 days for latest features
antiPatterns: Hardcoding secrets directly in wrangler.jsonc - use wrangler secret instead
Skipping wrangler types after adding new bindings
Deploying without testing locally first using wrangler dev

faq.question: What version of wrangler should I use?
faq.answer: Use wrangler v4.x or later. Check with wrangler --version.

faq.question: Can I use TOML or JSON for config?
faq.answer: Prefer wrangler.jsonc (JSON with comments). Newer features are JSON-only.

faq.question: How do I test my Worker locally?
faq.answer: Run wrangler dev. Use wrangler dev --remote for Cloudflare bindings.

faq.question: Where should I store local development secrets?
faq.answer: Use .dev.vars file in your project root. Never commit this file.

faq.question: How do I rollback a bad deployment?
faq.answer: Use wrangler rollback or wrangler rollback <VERSION_ID>.

faq.question: Does Workers AI work locally?
faq.answer: No, Workers AI always runs remotely and incurs charges even in local dev.
